analysis.stackSummary:
This roster embraces a Chargers control game and builds around the roster spots that benefit from it. McConkey captain plus Herbert flex captures the core receiving production without requiring a second expensive Chargers wideout to hit.

The unusual part sits in the supporting pieces. Vidal, Dicker, and the Chargers defense all benefit from the same scoreboard. Sustained drives create kicker chances. Defensive pressure rises once Pittsburgh trails and must throw. Vidal adds points without needing a 25 carry outcome because the game flow creates efficient touches.

The single Steelers piece is chosen for minimum cost and maximum leverage. Wilson functions as the only Pittsburgh access point needed because the Steelers score only ten. A cheap receiver with one meaningful play beats spending on a full Steelers stack that never arrives.
analysis.uniquenessNotes:
Five Chargers creates strong correlation, but the specific mix matters. K plus DST plus RB sits away from the more common double receiver builds.

McConkey captain keeps the ceiling tied to Herbert’s production while still separating from Herbert captain constructions.

construction.buildType: Chargers heavy control game capture with WR captain plus QB, reinforced by DST and K

stackMeta.primaryLever: Align every Chargers slot with the same positive script
stackMeta.secondaryLever: Avoid spending on Pittsburgh volume, use a single cheap receiver instead
